PSALM LXIIL Dawid’s desire for God. A Psalm of David, when he was in the wilderness of Judah. GOD,-thou: art my God; early will i seek thee; my cout thirsteth for thee, my flesh longeth for

This shows, that though he was driven from there, he did not cease to seek the Holy One, blessed be He. “My soul thirsteth for thee”, because my soul and my body yearn for thee that I may appear before thee and I am not able, being in a dry and weary land where no water is; for so any land is called where the Shekinah does not abide.’

This shows, that though he was driven from there, he did not cease to seek the Holy One, blessed be He. “My soul thirsteth for thee”, because my soul and my body yearn for thee that I may appear before thee and I am not able, being in a dry and weary land where no water is; for so any land is called where the Shekinah does not abide.’

22 And we ask thee, Holy Father, that thy servants may go forth from this house armed with thy power, and that thy name may be upon them, and thy glory be round about them, and thine angels have charge over them;

22 And we ask thee, Holy Father, that thy servants may go forth from this house armed with thy power, and that thy name may be upon them, and thy glory be round about them, and thine angels have charge over them;

JUDAH. Thou art as thy name,55That is, thy name Judah means praise. and thy brethren shall so praise thee.56The verse literally reads: Judah thou, thy brethren shall praise thee. I.E interprets thus: Judah thou art as thy name and thy brethren shall so praise thee. KJV: Every one that is found shall be thrust through; and every one that is joined [unto them] shall fall by the sword.

heads we read: ‘ And whereas thou didst see that the great head disappeared—[one of them shall die upon his bed, but yet with pain. But as for the two who remain the sword shall devour them. For the sword of the one shall devour him that was with him; nevertheless this one also shall fall by the sword in the last days’).
